prompt: The mutable component breaks the record's value semantics. What is the bug?
choices: Record components cannot hold List types
The List reference is shared; mutating vals after construction also mutates t.values(), breaking immutability; the compact constructor should make a defensive copy: values = List.copyOf(values)
Records auto-copy mutable components in the generated constructor
vals.add("c") throws UnsupportedOperationException because record fields are final
explanation: Records store the reference, not a copy. The caller retains a reference to the same List, so vals.add("c") mutates the data inside the record — t.values().size() becomes 3. Fix: defensive copy in the compact constructor: values = List.copyOf(values); to make the record truly immutable.
